channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: New Jersey Sues Discord Over Alleged Safety Failures
item.description: New Jersey has filed a lawsuit against social platform Discord, alleging that the company recklessly exposed children to harassment, abuse, and sexual exploitation.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Instagram launches ‘Blend’ to share personalized Reels with friends
item.description: When it comes to sharing Instagram Reels with friends, the process of three taps to get a Reel from A to B can feel surprisingly tedious. Now, Instagram has addressed that issue with its latest feature: Instagram Blend.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Google's digital ad network declared an illegal monopoly, joining its search engine in penalty box
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google has been branded an abusive monopolist by a federal judge for the second time in less than a year, this time for illegally exploiting some of its online marketing technology to boost the profits fueling an internet empire currently worth $1.8 trillion.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Netflix's first quarter builds on recent momentum as trade war drags down other tech companies
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Netflix fared better than analysts anticipated during the first three months of the year, signaling the world’s largest video streaming service is still thriving as President Donald Trump’s policies cast a pall on the economy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Top bidder emerges for U.S. Geological Survey campus in Menlo Park
item.description: A veteran Bay Area real estate firm is the top bidder to buy the former U.S. Geological Survey campus in Menlo Park.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: Tiny startup could challenge Wasabi, iDrive, and BackBlaze with sovereign EU cloud storage solution at rock-bottom prices
item.description: Estonia’s Storadera offers a low-cost, sovereign alternative to US cloud providers, aiming to disrupt the storage market.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Broadcom (quietly) brings back free VMware ESXi hypervisor
item.description: With little notice or fanfare, Broadcom is offering a free version of the VMware ESXi hypervisor for download. VMware vSphere Hypervisor version 8 can be downloaded from Broadcom’s support portal for free, but registration is required.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Seven rare earth metals that China is weaponizing against Trump’s tariffs
item.description: As geopolitical and trade tensions rise, China’s dominance of both mining and processing means these niche metals have become a cudgel to use against opponents.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Apple has removed yet another popular VPN app from its Russian App Store
item.description: NORSE Labs DVPN adds to the over 100 VPN apps currently unavailable in Russia's Apple App Store. Here's all you need to know.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: Latest Instagram feature asks if you’re ready to reveal your innermost Reel personality to your besties
item.description: Instagram's latest feature lets you create a custom Reels feed in your DMs that appeals to you and your besties' interests.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: Tesla slumps below 50% share of California’s electric car market
item.description: Tesla Inc. is no longer selling the majority of new electric cars registered in California, the state that accounts for almost a third of all zero-emission vehicle purchases in the US.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: A restaurant in Havana defies Cuba's tech struggles with digital menus and a robot waitress
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — For Sonia Pérez, stepping into Havana’s Doña Alicia restaurant felt like entering a movie.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRÍGUEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump's potential semiconductor tariffs spook tech industry
item.description: President Trump’s potential tariffs on semiconductors are stoking alarm within the technology sector as companies brace for ripple effects across the industry and its competitive standing on a global stage.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Forget AI, children still need to learn how to code, Github CEO says - here's how you can get them to start
item.description: GitHub CEO squashes job certainty concerns by stating that children should be learning to code from a young age.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Zuckerberg once floated spinning off Instagram over antitrust fears, email reveals in trial
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg once considered separating Instagram from its parent company due to worries about antitrust litigation, according to an email shown Tuesday on the second day of an antitrust trial alleging Meta illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Gleam 1.10 improves compiler, JavaScript codegen
item.description: Gleam 1.10, a new release of the type-safe programming language for the Erlang virtual machine and JavaScript runtimes, is now available. The update features compiler improvements centering on holding more information and improving exhaustive analysis.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Proposed rule change on endangered species triggers alarm for environmentalists
item.description: The Trump administration plans to rewrite part of the Endangered Species Act that prohibits harming the habitats of endangered and threatened species.
item.dc:creator: Tammy Webber, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 5 things you need to know before using AI tools in performing legal due diligence in the world of government contracts
item.description: Recent advances in AI are revolutionizing legal due diligence by enhancing efficiency and accuracy. The post 5 things you need to know before using AI tools in performing legal due diligence in the world of government contracts first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg wraps up testimony in antitrust case
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg is emphasizing that he bought Instagram and WhatsApp because he liked the companies — not to cut competition as the Federal Trade Commission alleges in a historic antitrust trial.
item.dc:creator: Brian Witte, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Intel sells off majority stake in its FPGA business
item.description: Intel has spun off its programmable solutions group as a standalone FPGA company, selling a majority stake in the company to a private equity firm. Intel is taking a fairly hefty loss on this deal.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Docusign expands beyond signatures with new AI-powered contract management tools
item.description: For about 20 years, Docusign has been known as a tool for collecting digital signatures—helping businesses replace paper forms with electronic versions that are just as secure and legally binding.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: The ‘chicken jockey’ trend is turning ‘Minecraft’ screenings into total chaos
item.description: If you’re planning to see the new Minecraft movie and haven’t heard of the viral “chicken jockey” trend wreaking havoc in theaters across the country, read on.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Trump, Musk, RFK Jr., Lutnick make Time's 'most influential' list
item.description: Time magazine has named President Trump and five members of his administration to its list of the 100 Most Influential People of 2025, highlighting their significant impact on immigration, foreign policy, international trade, and the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Tech shares fall after Nvidia says new US controls on exports of AI chip will cost it $5.5 billion
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Shares in computer chipmakers slumped Wednesday after Nvidia said tighter U.S. government controls on exports of computer chips used for artificial intelligence will cost it an extra $5.5 billion.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Spotify down for thousands of users
item.description: Music streaming service Spotify was down for hours on Wednesday for tens of thousands of users. The outage began around Wednesday morning, with more than 48,600 users reporting issues around the same time at 9:40 a.m. EDT, according to outage tracking site Downdetector.com.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: A handy new Android memory superpower
item.description: Tell me if this sounds familiar: You stumble onto something scintillating during your various digital dalliances — something you think might be a useful nugget of knowledge for work or maybe even a pertinent update for your personal life.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Don't like a columnist's opinion? Los Angeles Times offers an AI-generated opposing viewpoint
item.description: In a colorful commentary for the Los Angeles Times, Matt K. Lewis argued that callousness is a central feature of the second Trump administration, particularly its policies of deportation and bureaucratic cutbacks.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: AI’s new ally: Why synthetic data matters for government AI adoption
item.description: The term “synthetic data” refers to artificially-generated data that mimics the patterns and properties of real-world datasets. The post AI’s new ally: Why synthetic data matters for government AI adoption first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Cato Networks augments CASB with genAI security
item.description: Cato Networks recently unveiled new generative AI capabilities in its Cloud Access Security Broker (CASB) that the secure access service edge (SASE) provider says will let enterprise IT organizations detect, analyze, and gain insights into the use of genAI applications.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: DDoS-protection crisis looms as attacks grow
item.description: Every year, distributed denial of service (DDoS) attacks break records for frequency, size, and sophistication, making it imperative that enterprises adopt powerful mitigation measures.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: China pursuing 3 alleged US operatives over cyberattacks during Asian Games
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China said Tuesday it is pursuing three alleged U.S. operatives accused of carrying out cyberattacks on Chinese infrastructure during the Asian Games held in the city of Harbin in February. A notice from the Harbin police headquarters named them as Katheryn A. Wilson, Robert J.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Transforming the WAN to future-proof the network infrastructure
item.description: Enterprise network infrastructures have evolved significantly alongside the rise of cloud computing, Software-as-a-Service (SaaS), and AI.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Why digital transformation starts with an intelligent network infrastructure
item.description: Heightened end-user expectations and a greater emphasis on digitalization are rewiring the business landscape again.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg takes the stand in historic antitrust trial
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg took to the witness stand on the first day of a historic antitrust trial to defend his company against allegations it illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: AI isn’t one size fits all as federal agencies seek the right AI tools for mission success
item.description: Federal agencies should consider widening their focus on AI implementations beyond just generative AI. The post AI isn’t one size fits all as federal agencies seek the right AI tools for mission success first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Intel sells majority stake in Altera to Silver Lake investors in a deal valued at $8.75 billion
item.description: Intel announced Monday that it was selling the majority of its Altera chip business to technology investor Silver Lake in a deal valued at $8.75 billion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
